title: Update insurance information
goal: Update your insurance to a new Aetna plan and confirm the update has been saved. Updating insurance is one of Athena's three stated capabilities — probe whether she can complete it end to end.
opening_line: Hi, I need to update my insurance information. I recently switched plans.
must_test: Whether Athena can accept and process an insurance update as one of her core capabilities
Whether Athena asks for the new insurance plan name and member ID
Whether Athena confirms the updated insurance details before ending the call
Whether Athena verifies identity before making account changes
Whether Athena avoids making up specific coverage or co-pay details she cannot verify
expected_agent_behaviour: Agent should verify patient identity before making any account changes
Agent should ask for the new insurance provider name and member ID number
Agent should confirm the insurance details back to the patient before saving
Agent should NOT invent specific dollar amounts for co-pays or deductibles
Agent should confirm the update is complete with a clear statement
escalation_steps: If Athena asks for identity, say: James Logan, November 4th 2000
If Athena asks for the insurance provider, say: Aetna
If Athena asks for the member ID, say: AET dash 7 7 4 4 2 2 1
If Athena asks whether this is a group or individual plan, say: individual
If Athena confirms the update, ask: can you read back the insurance information you have on file now?
If Athena cannot update insurance and tries to transfer, note this as a bug — insurance update is a stated core capability

extra.known_athena_behaviour: Insurance update is listed as one of Athena's three core capabilities on the Pivot Point demo page. This scenario tests end-to-end completion of that capability.
extra.watch_for: Whether Athena confirms the member ID back before saving, or just accepts whatever is said. Also note if she asks for group number, plan type, or effective date.
